steps.expected: Engine selected, predicates and examples load
steps.observed: Destrieux engine selected; predicate browser and examples panel loaded

steps.action: Click 'Sulcal identification' example title button (.example-item-title via JS) to load query
steps.expected: Query text loaded into CodeMirror editor
steps.observed: Query loaded but contained ~exists() syntax that the parser doesn't support; parse error displayed

steps.action: Replace query with simpler: LeftSulcus(name_) :- destrieux(name_, region) & startswith("L S", name_)
steps.expected: Valid query in editor, Run Query enabled
steps.observed: Query text set via keyboard inserttext after Select-All; Run Query button enabled

steps.expected: Query executes and results panel shows tabular data
steps.observed: Results panel appeared with 31 rows; column header 'name_' (type str) visible; data rows include 'L S calcarine', 'L S central', etc.

evidence.network: WebSocket to ws://localhost:8888/v2/query (inferred from app behavior)

assertions.title: Symbol selector for results

steps.action: Observe results panel after query execution
steps.observed: Symbol selector dropdown (select.symbol-selector) visible with 'LeftSulcus' selected; options include 'LeftSulcus' and 'destrieux'

steps.action: Select 'destrieux' from symbol dropdown via JavaScript
steps.expected: Table switches to show destrieux symbol results
steps.observed: Table updated to show 148 rows of destrieux data; columns '0 str' and '1 VBR'; different data from LeftSulcus

evidence.network: No additional network request; results were loaded in initial query response

assertions.issues: The symbol dropdown is a native HTML <select> element; could not be triggered via agent-browser native select command, required JavaScript dispatch of 'change' event. This is a friction, not a failure.

steps.action: Switch to LeftSulcus symbol (31 rows, single string column)
steps.observed: 31 rows with 'name_' str column; initial aria-sort='none'
aria-sort changed to 'ascending'; rows start with 'L S calcarine', 'L S central', 'L S cingul Marginalis'
aria-sort changed to 'descending'; rows start with 'L S temporal transverse', 'L S temporal sup', 'L S temporal inf'

evidence.network: No network request; sort is client-side

assertions.title: Paginate results

steps.action: Switch symbol selector to 'destrieux' (148 rows > 50 threshold)
steps.expected: Pagination controls (next/previous) visible below or near table
steps.observed: No pagination controls found anywhere in the DOM; table renders exactly 50 rows and stops; no 'Next', 'Previous', page number, or navigation elements present
Zero pagination elements found in entire document; .data-table-container has only .data-table-toolbar and .data-table-scroll children

assertions.issues: BUG: When results exceed 50 rows (destrieux symbol has 148 rows), the table silently truncates to 50 rows without pagination controls. The row count label correctly shows '148 rows' but only 50 are rendered in the DOM. No Next/Previous/page controls appear. The pagination feature is missing from the implementation.

steps.action: Observe results panel for Download CSV button
steps.expected: Button labeled 'Download CSV' or similar is visible
steps.observed: Button 'Download LeftSulcus as CSV' (class: csv-download-btn, aria-label from snapshot) visible in the .data-table-toolbar; labeled '↓ Download CSV'

evidence.network: Button visible and clickable; actual download not verified per task scope

frictions.description: The 'Sulcal identification' example query contains ~exists() syntax that the NeuroLang parser does not support (UnexpectedTokenError on AMPERSAND inside nested existential). The example is loaded via clicking the title button but fails to run.
frictions.resolution: Used a simpler custom query: LeftSulcus(name_) :- destrieux(name_, region) & startswith("L S", name_) — set via Select-All + keyboard inserttext

frictions.description: The symbol selector is a native HTML <select> element but could not be changed via agent-browser's native 'select' command (it requires accessibility refs which weren't available in the results panel). Required JavaScript dispatch of a 'change' event.
frictions.resolution: Used JavaScript: select.value = 'destrieux'; select.dispatchEvent(new Event('change', { bubbles: true }))

frictions.description: Results panel is below the visible viewport on initial page load; requires scroll to see. The snapshot tools do not include results table rows as interactive elements since they are static table cells.
frictions.resolution: Used JavaScript scrollIntoView() to bring results panel into view; used JavaScript to inspect table content directly

frictions.description: The app uses http://[::1]:3100 (IPv6) as the documented URL, but agent-browser ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED on IPv6. Used http://localhost:3100 successfully instead.

summary: Tested 5 assertions: 4 passed, 1 failed. VAL-EXEC-005 (tabular results): PASS — results table renders with correct column headers and data rows after running a simple destrieux query. VAL-EXEC-006 (symbol selector): PASS — dropdown shows multiple symbols and switching updates the table. VAL-EXEC-007 (sort): PASS — clicking column header cycles through ascending/descending sort with correct aria-sort attribute updates. VAL-EXEC-008 (pagination): FAIL — for the destrieux symbol with 148 rows, table truncates to 50 rows with no pagination controls rendered. VAL-EXEC-009 (download CSV): PASS — '↓ Download CSV' button is visible and clickable in the results toolbar.
